title: material: align egui material structure and port slint ui files
description:

- Port remaining material components/items from Slint and expand icon coverage.
Definition of Done:
- src/material mirrors the source ui folder structure closely enough that components, items, icons, and styling code live under material.
- Existing egui material code is moved or re-exported from the aligned structure.
- Every material-1.0/ui/**/*.slint file has a tracked child task and corresponding egui implementation or explicit follow-up.
- All egui asset icons are referenced from the aligned material icon registry.
Notes:
- Start by moving existing code from ui_icons and ui_style into material.
- Do not commit before user verification.

title: audio_player: restore runtime playback and slider ownership gate in egui
description:
Goal:
Restore functional audio playback with runtime-driven position updates and prevent feedback loops between the audio actor and the position slider in egui.
Definition of Done:
- Opening an audio file does not crash the UI.
- Play/pause produces audio output when a playable file is opened.
- The position slider is driven by the audio player while playing.
- While the user is dragging/repositioning the slider, actor-driven position updates are gated and do not feed back into the slider.
- On seek commit, the audio player receives the requested position and actor ownership resumes after acknowledgement.
- Focused specs cover the runtime poll and slider-ownership flow.
Notes:
- User explicitly asked to guard against feedback loops between audio playback position updates and the UI slider state machine.

title: ui: investigate ShellHost::new stack overflow
description:
Goal:
Identify and fix the Slint item tree initialization causing stack overflow in ShellHost::new.
Definition of Done:
- Root cause identified (recursion or large stack frame).
- Fix applied so desktop app starts without stack overflow.
- Add regression note if needed.
Notes:
Stack trace shows overflow during choreo_components::ui::slint_generatedSplashScreen::InnerShellHost::item_array() before ShellHost::new() returns. Likely Slint component graph or large item array; consider bisection or larger stack test.
notes: Root cause: RefCell borrow during floor behavior activation (draw_floor_behavior bind in activate) caused panic and STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN. Fixed by splitting behavior activation into initialize/bind and binding after releasing the RefCell borrow; floor behaviors now bind via Rc<RefCell>.

title: android_egui: implement android bootstrap wiring
description:
Goal:
Implement Android egui bootstrap in apps/android_egui.

Definition of Done:
- android_main entrypoint performs platform bootstrap and launches egui app shell.
- Non-android fallback stubs remain compile-safe without behavior drift.
- cargo build/clippy/test checks pass for android_egui scope (or documented platform-limited verification).

Notes:
- Keep changes scoped to egui app and shared egui components only.
notes: Implemented Android egui bootstrap wiring in apps/android_egui: android_main now accepts winit AndroidApp, configures eframe NativeOptions event_loop_builder with EventLoopBuilderExtAndroid::with_android_app, and launches AppShellViewModel via an eframe::App wrapper. Kept a non-Android compile-safe android_main stub with no behavior side effects. Updated Cargo.toml with Android-target-specific eframe and winit dependencies. Verification: cargo build -p rchoreo_android_egui PASS; cargo clippy -p rchoreo_android_egui --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings PASS; cargo test -p rchoreo_android_egui PASS (0 tests). Platform limitation: cargo build -p rchoreo_android_egui --target aarch64-linux-android FAIL in this environment because rust target is not installed (E0463 core/std missing).

title: egui_apps: implement app bootstrap entrypoints
description:
Goal:
Implement production-ready bootstrap entrypoints for all egui apps in the workspace.

Definition of Done:
- Desktop, WASM, and Android egui app entrypoints initialize correctly and wire the shared app shell.
- Platform bootstrap follows idiomatic eframe/egui setup for each target.
- Each app target has passing build/test checks for its scope.

Notes:
- Do not change choreo_components (original Slint crate).

title: desktop_egui: implement native bootstrap wiring
description:
Goal:
Implement desktop egui bootstrap in apps/desktop_egui.

Definition of Done:
- main entrypoint configures eframe native options and launches app shell with stable app id/title.
- Startup/logging/theme bootstrap hooks are wired for desktop context.
- cargo build/clippy/test checks pass for desktop_egui scope.

Notes:
- Keep changes scoped to egui app and shared egui components only.
notes: Implemented desktop egui bootstrap wiring in apps/desktop_egui/src/main.rs: added stable APP_ID/APP_TITLE constants, configured eframe NativeOptions viewport with app_id/title, initialized startup logging via env_logger+log, and applied desktop theme bootstrap at app creation. Updated apps/desktop_egui/Cargo.toml with log/env_logger dependencies. Verification: cargo build -p rchoreo_desktop_egui (pass); cargo clippy -p rchoreo_desktop_egui --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings (pass; non-fatal incremental file-lock warnings observed from rustc); cargo test -p rchoreo_desktop_egui (pass, 0 tests).

title: wasm_egui: implement web bootstrap wiring
description:
Goal:
Implement WASM egui bootstrap in apps/wasm_egui.

Definition of Done:
- wasm start path mounts eframe web runner to canvas and launches app shell.
- Browser-specific init (panic/log/canvas lookup) is in place.
- cargo check/test for wasm_egui scope passes (or documented platform-limited verification).

Notes:
- Keep changes scoped to egui app and shared egui components only.
notes: Implemented wasm egui bootstrap in apps/wasm_egui: added eframe WebRunner startup via wasm_bindgen(start), browser diagnostics init (panic hook + console_log), and canvas lookup/validation before launch. Updated index.html with dedicated canvas and full-viewport sizing. Updated Cargo.toml with eframe + wasm diagnostics/runtime deps and required web-sys features. Verification: cargo check/build/test -p rchoreo_wasm_egui passed on host target; wasm target cargo check/build/test with --target wasm32-unknown-unknown all fail in this environment because rustc sysroot lacks wasm std/core (E0463), despite rustup reporting target installed.

title: timestamp_state_machine_egui: restore machine-owned timing and state semantics parity
description:
Goal: Match source timing and state semantics for pending seek synchronization.
Definition of Done: Pending seek timeout/age is machine-owned (Instant/clock abstraction equivalent) rather than caller-supplied now_seconds; snapshot exposes pending_seek_age_seconds parity; side-effect flags not present in source contract (pause_playback_requested/resume_playback_requested/last_actor_sample_accepted) are removed or moved to owning component adapter layer.
Notes: Source module computes seek age internally and returns bools from apply_event/complete_seek_commit instead of carrying UI-side effect flags in state.
notes:
Restored machine-owned timestamp timing/state semantics parity:
- Seek commit timing is now owned internally via Instant in TimestampOwnershipStateMachine (no external now_seconds inputs).
- Snapshot exposes pending_seek_age_seconds from internal elapsed timing.
- Removed non-source side-effect flags (pause_playback_requested/resume_playback_requested/last_actor_sample_accepted) by moving to bool return semantics on apply_event/complete_seek_commit.
- Added timeout-focused spec to validate machine-owned pending-seek aging behavior.
Verification:
- cargo build -p choreo_components_egui --lib (pass)
- cargo clippy -p choreo_components_egui --lib -- -D warnings (pass; tool emitted non-lint incremental warning)
- cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test timestamp_state_machine_tests -- --nocapture (pass)

title: timestamp_state_machine_egui: align public API to TimestampOwnershipStateMachine contract
description:

Definition of Done: Egui timestamp_state_machine exports machine-centric types and methods equivalent to source (PlaybackPhase, OwnershipPhase, TimestampEvent, TimestampStateSnapshot, TimestampOwnershipStateMachine with apply_event/set_playback_from_player/complete_seek_commit/snapshot APIs); reducer/action state is not the primary exported contract.
Notes: Current egui module exposes action/reducer/state files and does not provide source-equivalent machine API.
notes:
Aligned egui timestamp_state_machine public API to TimestampOwnershipStateMachine parity contract:
- Added machine-centric API in src/timestamp_state_machine/mod.rs: PlaybackPhase, OwnershipPhase, TimestampEvent, TimestampStateSnapshot, TimestampOwnershipStateMachine.
- Implemented apply_event, set_playback_from_player, complete_seek_commit, snapshot, set/is_adjusting_speed parity methods.
- Exported timestamp_state_machine module from crate root (src/lib.rs).
- Updated timestamp_state_machine tests to consume machine API directly.
Verification:

description:

- Original crates/choreo_components must not be modified.

title: timestamp_state_machine_egui: remove non-parity GUI component surface
description:

Restore timestamp_state_machine parity by removing GUI rendering/actions from crates/choreo_components_egui/src/timestamp_state_machine.

- Ensure timestamp_state_machine component remains non-UI state-machine logic only.
- Verify no egui controls remain for timestamp_state_machine component scope.

- Current egui mismatch includes ui.rs heading/labels/button (Reset).
notes: Parity already satisfied: crates/choreo_components_egui/src/timestamp_state_machine/mod.rs is non-UI and matches baseline state-machine-only scope. Confirmed no timestamp_state_machine ui.rs/UI control surface remains and ran cargo test -p choreo_components_egui timestamp_state_machine (5 passing).

title: timestamp_state_machine_egui: align exported API with core machine contract
description:
Goal:
Port non-UI parity so egui timestamp_state_machine exposes the same machine-oriented contract as the original component.
Definition of Done:
- Provide TimestampOwnershipStateMachine-style API with methods/events/snapshot parity consumable directly by other components.
- Align event and state semantics with original contract (including pending seek age based on machine-owned timing and actor-sample acceptance behavior).
- Remove reducer/action/state as the primary component contract in parity path.
Notes:
- Original baseline: crates/choreo_components/src/timestamp_state_machine/mod.rs.
- Current egui module re-implements logic through action/reducer/state files and diverges in API surface (extra UI-oriented flags, external now_seconds inputs).
notes: Verified parity contract already implemented: crates/choreo_components_egui/src/timestamp_state_machine exposes machine/event/snapshot API aligned with crates/choreo_components/src/timestamp_state_machine/mod.rs; no reducer/action/state files remain under this component path. Prior targeted parity check cargo test -p choreo_components_egui timestamp_state_machine passed with 5 tests.

title: shell: restore helper and material-scheme applier parity in egui
description:
Goal:
Match original shell non-UI integration surface from crates/choreo_components/src/shell in crates/choreo_components_egui/src/shell.
Definition of Done:
- Add parity equivalents for shell helpers such as app title/host creation contracts as required by egui architecture.
- Provide shell-level material-scheme application integration equivalent to original ShellMaterialSchemeApplier responsibilities.
- Ensure downstream settings/theme flows can consume shell parity APIs without reducer-only substitutions.
- Add/adjust specs for shell material scheme application path and exported API parity.
Notes:
- Discovered during parity audit in RChoreoApp-jptn.16.
- Original shell module currently exports ShellMaterialSchemeApplier plus app_title/create_shell_host helpers; egui shell module does not.
notes: Validated shell helper + material-scheme parity surface exists in crates/choreo_components_egui/src/shell: app_title/create_shell_host exports plus ShellMaterialSchemeApplier host contract and tests. Verification: cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test shell_tests (pass) and cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test main_ui_exports_spec (pass).

title: slider_with_ticks: restore component architecture parity (view-model + behavior + DI)
description:
Goal:
Align choreo_components_egui slider_with_ticks component architecture with original choreo_components slider_with_ticks.
Definition of Done:
- Provide slider_with_ticks component as injectable view-model + behavior surface (minimum, maximum, value, tick_values, tick_color, is_enabled), matching original ownership and clamping semantics.
- Preserve lifecycle behavior parity (activation path and behavior composition) instead of a standalone reducer-only placeholder API.
- Keep module boundaries parity-focused so slider_with_ticks remains a reusable component consumed by higher-level audio UI.
Notes:
- Follow-up from parity audit ticket RChoreoApp-jptn.17.
- Original reference: crates/choreo_components/src/slider_with_ticks/*.rs.
notes: Implemented slider_with_ticks architecture parity in choreo_components_egui: added nject injectable wiring to SliderWithTicksViewModel (inject behavior list) and SliderWithTicksBehavior (transient inject constructor), and added reusable slider_with_ticks::ui module to keep component boundaries reusable for higher-level consumers. Added spec coverage for tick-range filtering via visible_tick_fractions.

title: slider_with_ticks: implement control semantics parity for ticked seek slider
description:
Goal:
Port slider-with-ticks control semantics from original stack into egui usage.
Definition of Done:
- Render tick marks as track-aligned visual markers (not label list placeholders), honoring range bounds and tick color.
- Preserve slider interaction semantics expected by audio flow, including enabled-state gating and value clamping behavior.
- Ensure integration surface supports drag/seek interaction parity needed by audio player (start/change/complete flow) and remove placeholder-only controls (heading/init button/debug labels).
- Use egui-material3 mappings where Material-style control treatment applies.
Notes:
- Follow-up from parity audit ticket RChoreoApp-jptn.17.
- Coordinate with existing audio player parity tickets to avoid duplicate implementation paths.
notes: Ported ticked seek-slider semantics to componentized slider_with_ticks::ui and wired audio_player/ui.rs to consume its interactions. Tick marks are rendered as track-aligned markers with range filtering and explicit tick color input; drag lifecycle is surfaced as DragStarted/ValueChanged(is_dragging)/DragCompleted and mapped to audio actions (preview/seek/complete) for parity flow.

title: scenes: restore open/save/navigation action-flow parity in egui

Restore scenes action-flow parity between original scenes view model/behaviors and crates/choreo_components_egui/src/scenes action surface.

- Add missing scenes actions/state for settings navigation and dancer-settings navigation parity.
- Wire UI triggers for open choreography and save choreography actions (currently not emitted from ui.rs).
- Preserve enablement semantics for save/settings/dancer-navigation controls.
- Ensure parity for downstream side effects expected by scenes flow (selected-scene change propagation, reload/redraw/open-audio/close-audio request outputs) remains externally observable from state/output channels.
Notes:
- Discovered during parity audit of RChoreoApp-jptn.14.
- Source parity references: scenes_pane_view.slint, scenes_info.slint, scenes_view_model.rs, open_choreo_behavior.rs, save_choreo_behavior.rs.

title: scenes: port Material3 list/search/action control parity in egui
description:
Goal:
Bring crates/choreo_components_egui/src/scenes/ui.rs to control and Material3 parity with crates/choreo_components/src/scenes/{scenes_pane_view.slint,scene_item_view.slint,scene_search_bar.slint}.

title: time_egui: remove non-parity GUI component surface
description:
Goal:
Restore time component parity by removing GUI rendering/actions from crates/choreo_components_egui/src/time.
Definition of Done:
- Remove or isolate time ui.rs rendering from component parity path.
- Ensure time component itself remains non-UI utility/service scope matching original intent.
- Verify no egui controls remain for time component scope.
Notes:
- Original baseline crates/choreo_components/src/time/mod.rs has no UI.
- Current egui mismatch includes ui.rs heading/labels/buttons (Now/Parse input).
notes: Parity already satisfied: crates/choreo_components_egui/src/time contains only non-UI utility/service logic (SystemClock + parse/format helpers) with no ui.rs or egui controls in component scope, matching baseline intent. Verified with cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test time_tests (11 passing). Full crate-wide test selection currently has unrelated external failures in floor_public_api_parity_spec.

title: time_egui: align clock/parsing API parity with core time module
description:
Goal:
Port non-UI time abstraction parity so egui component matches original time module contract and platform semantics.
Definition of Done:
- Provide SystemClock::now_utc parity (including wasm behavior) instead of local reducer-only nanoseconds tracking.
- Expose parse_timestamp_seconds and format_seconds through a non-UI module contract equivalent to original.
- Remove dependence on UI reducer/action/state as the time component contract.
Notes:
- Original baseline: crates/choreo_components/src/time/mod.rs.
- Current egui module duplicates parse/format but diverges in now-time abstraction and wraps logic in UI-oriented state/reducer APIs.
notes: Aligned time API semantics to core time module contract: SystemClock::now_utc keeps wasm performance-origin behavior and matches non-annotated method signature style used in baseline. Confirmed non-UI time helpers remain exported from time module and validated with cargo build -p choreo_components_egui, cargo clippy -p choreo_components_egui --lib --all-features -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test time_tests (11 passing).

title: preferences_egui: port Preferences trait and platform backend parity
description:

Port non-UI preferences parity so egui component matches original abstraction and platform-backed behavior.
Definition of Done:
- Expose Preferences-style contract (get/set string/bool, remove) consumable by other components.
- Provide parity implementations for in-memory and platform-backed persistence (file for native, localStorage for wasm) including platform aliasing behavior.
- Provide shared wrapper semantics equivalent to original SharedPreferences usage.
- Remove reliance on reducer pending-write model as component contract.
Notes:
- Original baseline: crates/choreo_components/src/preferences/{types.rs,in_memory.rs,file.rs,wasm.rs,shared.rs,mod.rs}.
- Current egui module is action/reducer/state centric and lacks equivalent trait/backend exports.

Preferences component contract remains non-UI persistence only via mod.rs exports (types/in_memory/shared/platform backends). Validated with cargo build -p choreo_components_egui and cargo test -p choreo_components_egui --test preferences_tests (4 passing). crate-level clippy currently fails on unrelated pre-existing issue in crates/choreo_components_egui/src/choreography_settings/ui.rs (collapsible_if).

title: observability: enforce OTEL no-op when not activated
description: Goal: enforce OTEL no-op when not activated. Plan: 1) make observability spec feature-aware for otel and non-otel builds, 2) set tracing gate default to disabled and only enable explicitly from host init, 3) remove endpoint reachability preflight and keep fast guards in capture/start span, 4) run build/clippy/test quality gates for touched projects.

title: floor: prevent single-touch pan from preempting two-finger gesture
description:
Goal:
Ensure two-finger touch pan/zoom remains active and is not overridden by single-finger pointer gesture handling.
Definition of Done:
- Gesture arbitration uses explicit state to separate pointer pan from touch multi-gesture mode.
- While multi-touch is active, pointer drag does not mutate viewport transform.
- Specs cover regression where first/single touch takes over during two-finger interaction.
Notes:
- Apply fix in GestureHandlingBehavior with minimal side effects.

Keep behavior unchanged; refactor test organization only.

- Relevant files: crates/choreo_components/ui/scenes_pane_view.slint, crates/choreo_components/ui/main_page.slint, crates/choreo_components/ui/shell_host.slint (content_index), crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/main_page_binding.rs (on_scenes_navigate_*), crates/choreo_components/src/scenes/scenes_view_model.rs (navigate handlers), crates/choreo_components/src/scenes/scenes_provider.rs (behaviors), crates/choreo_components/src/settings/* and crates/choreo_components/src/dancers/*.

title: scenes: fix open choreography button
description:
Goal:
- Make the Open button in the scenes control open a .choreo file on desktop and web.

Definition of Done:
- Clicking Open shows a file dialog on desktop; on web uses a web picker.
- Selected file loads into global state and refreshes scenes.

Notes:
- Open action is wired via scenes callbacks and OpenChoreoBehavior.
- Relevant files: crates/choreo_components/ui/scenes_pane_view.slint, crates/choreo_components/ui/main_page.slint, crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/main_page_binding.rs (on_scenes_open_choreo), crates/choreo_components/src/scenes/scenes_view_model.rs (open_choreo), crates/choreo_components/src/scenes/open_choreo_behavior.rs (pick_choreo_path, is_choreo_file, load_choreo), apps/desktop/src/main.rs (pick_choreo_path), apps/wasm/src/lib.rs (pick_choreo_path None).

title: web: implement file pickers for open image/audio
description:
Goal:
- Make Main Open Image and Open Audio work in the web (WASM) build.

Definition of Done:
- Web build can prompt for a file and load the selected image/audio (no silent no-op).
- Uses web-friendly file picker and integrates with existing open_audio/open_image flows.

Notes:
- Current WASM app sets pickers to None, so callbacks no-op.
- Relevant files: apps/wasm/src/lib.rs (MainPageActionHandlers), crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/main_page_binding.rs (open_image/open_audio dispatch), crates/choreo_components/src/nav_bar/nav_bar_view_model.rs, crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/open_audio_behavior.rs, crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/open_image_behavior.rs, crates/choreo_components/ui/main_page.slint.

title: choreo_components/floor: use web_time Instant
description:
Goal:
Avoid std::time::Instant panics on wasm by using web_time.
Definition of Done:
Floor behavior uses web_time::Instant and wasm app no longer panics on Instant::now.
Notes:
Update dependencies as needed.

title: wasm: fix SystemTime::now panic
description:
Goal:
Avoid SystemTime::now on wasm to prevent runtime panic.
Definition of Done:
WASM uses a safe time source and app loads without panic.
Notes:
Likely in choreo_models defaults.

title: wasm: build and run local server
description:
Goal:
Build the WASM app and serve it locally.
Definition of Done:
wasm-pack build succeeds and a local http server is started.
Notes:
Browser launch may require user action.

title: choreo_components: remove rxrust usage
description:

Remove rxrust dependency and related code from choreo_components in favor of simpler disposal patterns.
Definition of Done:
rxrust dependency removed, no rxrust imports remain, and build/tests pass.
Notes:
Replace or remove SubscriptionDisposable and any exports if unused.

title: ui: configure wasm/android entrypoints
description:
Goal:
Ensure WASM and Android projects are configured appropriately for stack limitations and UI threading.
Definition of Done:
WASM/Android entrypoints updated with correct configuration and documentation comments as needed.
Notes:
Align with Slint constraints for those targets.

title: ui: run Slint UI on dedicated thread stack
description:

Move Slint UI execution to a dedicated thread with explicit stack size to avoid stack overflow from object count.
Definition of Done:
UI startup runs on a named thread with configured stack size and app still launches normally.
Notes:
Update main entry point to spawn/join UI thread as needed.

title: ui: diagnose drawer host recursion
description:
Goal:
- Identify why uncommenting drawer host causes recursion/crash.
Definition of Done:

- If found, propose fix or minimal repro details.
Notes:

title: floor: avoid view_model borrow in rx subscriptions
description:
Goal:
- Stop runtime borrow panics when pointer/touch subscriptions fire while the FloorCanvasViewModel is mutably borrowed by the adapter.

Definition of Done:
- Subscriptions no longer borrow the view model during event dispatch.
- Pointer and touch behaviors still function with correct floor bounds/transform data.
- Tests/build pass for the touched crates.

Notes:
- Adapter emits pointer events while holding a mutable borrow; current subscription closures borrow view_model, triggering RefCell borrow panics.

title: choreo_components: split behavior activation into initialize/bind and pre-adapter init
description: Goal: Refactor behavior activation to separate initialize/bind and ensure initialize runs before adapter attachment. Definition of Done: Behavior trait split applied; view models initialize before adapter wiring; adapters updated as needed; all impacted files adjusted. Notes: Use split activation + pre-adapter init approach.
notes: Behavior trait split into initialize/bind; floor view model now binds behaviors after releasing mutable borrow and before adapter attachment. Floor behaviors moved subscriptions to bind. View models now call initialize.

title: floor: split canvas rendering into dedicated egui items
description:
Goal:
Refactor the floor egui control so the root floor canvas composes dedicated egui items/widgets for each rendered concern instead of owning every paint pass directly.

Definition of Done:
- Keep crates/choreo_components/src/floor/ui.rs::draw focused on canvas allocation, coordinate conversion, interaction collection, and ordered composition.
- Extract dedicated egui-facing items/widgets for floor background/grid/center marks, SVG overlay placeholder/bounds, from/to path splines, dancer markers with labels/selection, axis labels, header overlay, legend panel, selection rectangle, and placement hint.
- Preserve reducer-owned geometry primitives in FloorState; avoid moving layout/projection math from reducer.rs into paint-only widgets unless a focused geometry module is introduced.
- Preserve the existing layer order contract: background, grid lines, floor SVG, path segments, position circles, position numbers, selection segments, header overlay.
- Add or extend behavior-focused specs for the extracted item composition and keep existing floor render parity specs passing.
- cargo build -p choreo_components, cargo clippy -p choreo_components --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, and cargo nextest run -p choreo_components pass.

Notes:
Findings from inspection on 2026-06-16:
- crates/choreo_components/src/floor/ui.rs::draw currently owns allocation, SetLayout/DrawFloor emission, event collection, color role resolution, and all painter calls.
- ui.rs paints many separate concerns inline: canvas/floor fill, grid and center marks, SVG overlay bounds, solid to-path segments, dashed from-path segments, fallback numbered positions, rendered dancer markers with shortcut labels and selection rings, axis labels, selection rectangle, header overlay, legend panel, and placement hint.
- reducer.rs already computes most render data as primitives on FloorState: floor/background/header/legend/svg rects, grid_lines, center_mark_segments, path_segments, dashed_path_segments, rendered_positions, position_circles, position_labels, axis_labels, legend_entries, selection_segments, and layer_order.
- FloorLayer exists in state.rs and is recomputed in reducer.rs, but ui.rs does not currently use layer_order as a composition driver; it hard-codes paint order.
- Existing coverage in crates/choreo_components/tests/floor/render_layer_parity_spec.rs checks layer order, header binding, legend layout, and scene projection into labels/legend/paths. That should be preserved and can anchor the refactor.
- Suggested split, consistent with docs/egui.md: keep mod.rs thin; add focused floor UI submodules such as canvas_item.rs, path_item.rs, dancer_item.rs, axis_label_item.rs, header_item.rs, legend_item.rs, placement_hint_item.rs, and possibly tokens.rs/geometry.rs if repeated paint metrics need ownership.
notes:
Implemented floor rendering split into dedicated egui item modules (canvas, paths, dancers, labels, selection, header, legend, placement hint) and moved floor_view_model.rs implementation to runtime.rs with FloorRuntime as the primary type. Validation: cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed; focused floor provider/render/runtime specs passed. Required all-target clippy is blocked by existing test macro warnings outside this change, and full cargo test is blocked by existing choreo_main timestamp/floor gesture assertions.
Follow-up fix: floor reducer now derives rendered dancer label color from the final rendered fill using black/white contrast and substitutes visible fallback fill/border colors when a dancer source fill or border is fully transparent, so dancer circles render filled instead of hollow. Added regression coverage in render_layer_parity_spec::draw_floor_renders_dancer_fills_and_contrast_text_colors. Validation: new spec passed; render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test lint failures; cargo nextest remains unavailable.
Follow-up fix: dancer source colors are now projected raw from choreo_main into FloorState, so source_positions.fill_color is the dancer model color and border_color is the role color. Transparency is applied once in the floor reducer. Added projection assertions for dancer fill/role border colors. Validation: choreo_main floor_projection_spec passed; floor render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed.
Follow-up fix: legend panel and positions-at-side labels now use the floor transformation matrix for overlay geometry. Legend panel width, margin, y-position, row height, padding, icon sizes, strokes, and text sizes scale with state.transformation_matrix; viewport clamping was removed so it pans with the floor. Side/top position label gaps and font sizes also scale with the same matrix. Added render-layer regression specs for legend pan/scale, legend height for explicit and scene-derived rows, and side label transform scaling. Validation: render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test lint failures; cargo nextest remains unavailable.
Implemented follow-up header transform fix after commit ffb35091. Header geometry now scales with floor transformation_matrix scale, header text offsets/font sizes scale with the same visual scale, and side-label mode reserves a transformed top label band so choreography/scene headers sit above top position labels. Added render parity specs for header transform scaling and non-overlap with top side-position labels. Validation: cargo test -p choreo_components --test floor_tests floor::render_layer_parity_spec -- --nocapture passes 14/14; cargo build -p choreo_components passes; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passes. Full clippy all-targets remains blocked by existing test lint debt; cargo nextest unavailable; cargo test -p choreo_components reaches existing timestamp_sync_spec failure.
Follow-up fix for menu bar floor actions: ResetFloorViewport now dispatches FloorAction::ResetViewport into floor_state before requesting redraw, so zoom/pan matrix resets. Empty Open floor SVG requests now resolve through the image picker before applying SVG state, so the selected path is stored/projected. Added floor SVG overlay parsing/rendering for line/polyline/polygon/simple M/L/H/V/Z path strokes, preserving SVG stroke colors and projecting segments onto transformed floor bounds below movement paths/dancers. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec passes 15/15; focused choreo_main open_svg_file_behavior_spec and non_ui_behavior_parity_spec pass; cargo build -p choreo_components passes; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passes. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test macro lint debt; cargo nextest unavailable; cargo test -p choreo_components still reaches existing timestamp_sync_spec failure.
Migrated floor SVG overlay rendering away from the custom SVG parser to egui's image pipeline. The selected SVG file is read into FloorState as bytes and FloorLayer::FloorSvg paints it with egui::Image::from_bytes(...).maintain_aspect_ratio(false).paint_at(...) over the transformed floor bounds. This uses the existing egui_extras svg feature/resvg loader installed by material::install_image_loaders, while preserving reset viewport and picker-path fixes. Removed custom svg_overlay parser module and segment state. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec passes 15/15; focused open_svg_file_behavior_spec and non_ui_behavior_parity_spec pass; cargo build -p choreo_components passes; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passes. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test macro lint debt; cargo nextest unavailable; cargo test -p choreo_components still reaches existing timestamp_sync_spec failure.
Follow-up SVG overlay bounds fix: egui-rendered SVG image is no longer constrained to the floor rectangle. FloorLayer::FloorSvg now uses overlay bounds derived from the full content area (layout_width_px x content_height_px), centered on the transformed floor center and scaled by zoom/transformation scale, so SVG content outside the floor rect remains visible while staying in the same transform space. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec passes 15/15; cargo build -p choreo_components passes; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passes.
Follow-up SVG intrinsic size fix: egui::Image::paint_at rasterizes to the supplied rect, so the floor SVG overlay rect must preserve the SVG source aspect. The floor reducer now derives source size from the SVG viewBox/width/height and fits that aspect inside the transformed content bounds, centered on the transformed floor center. Removed explicit maintain_aspect_ratio(false) from the image call. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec passes 15/15; cargo build -p choreo_components passes; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passes.
Follow-up fix: scene movement splines now carry per-dancer colors. FloorState has colored path segment primitives for solid to-paths and dashed from-paths; reducer derives them from the matched dancer fill color and mirrors plain LineSegment arrays for existing path command contracts; path_item paints colored scene paths while preserving fallback global strokes for simple position paths. Added render parity coverage for red/blue dancer colors on both path directions. Validation: focused scene path spec passed; floor render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test macro/lint debt; cargo nextest run -p choreo_components remains blocked by existing choreo_main::timestamp_sync_spec failure.
Follow-up refactor: floor SVG overlay painting moved from canvas_item.rs into dedicated floor_svg_item.rs. FloorLayer::FloorSvg now dispatches through floor_svg_item::draw_svg_overlay, keeping canvas_item focused on background/grid/floor primitives and leaving SVG source/bounds computation in reducer.rs. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test macro/lint debt; cargo nextest run -p choreo_components remains blocked by existing choreo_main::timestamp_sync_spec failure.
Follow-up fix: floor overlays now apply both transforms: the user zoom/pan matrix and an explicit layout fit scale derived from available window size. FloorState now stores layout_scale from the current pixels-per-meter relative to the default floor viewport; reducer geometry for header, legend, SVG bounds, and side-position label spacing uses layout_scale * transformation_matrix scale; axis/header/legend item font and token sizing use shared geometry scale helpers. Added resize regression coverage proving SVG, legend, header, and side-label gaps shrink/grow at the same ratio as the floor. Validation: floor render_layer_parity_spec group passed; cargo build -p choreo_components passed; cargo clippy -p choreo_components --lib -- -D warnings passed. Required all-target clippy remains blocked by existing test macro/lint debt; cargo nextest run -p choreo_components remains blocked by existing choreo_main::timestamp_sync_spec failure.

description: Goal: Document how to add i18n strings with regional-language translations instead of English fallbacks.\nDefinition of Done: docs page exists under docs/ and AGENTS.md links to it from the UI translation guidance.\nNotes: Requested after correcting material theme variant translations.

title: material-colors: map original component color usage
description:
# Material Color Planning Notes

Bead: RChoreoApp-beb0
Date: 2026-06-14
Scope: Planning only. No production code changes.

## Source Of Truth

- Original app: `D:\ChoreoApp`
- Rust port: `D:\RChoreoApp`
- Original dynamic scheme source: `ChoreoApp.Components\Settings\MaterialSchemeHelper.cs`
- Original dynamic color dictionary: `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\MaterialDesignColorsDictionary.cs`
- Rust dynamic scheme source: `crates\material3\src\styling\material_schemes.rs`
- Rust palette sync source: `crates\material3\src\styling\material_palette.rs`

The base dynamic color generation mostly maps 1:1:
- Both use default seed `#FF1976D2` / RGB `0x19,0x76,0xD2`.
- Both use Material `SchemeContent`.
- Both use `SpecVersion.Spec2025`.
- Both use `Platform.Phone`.
- Both use contrast `0.5`.

Important difference to verify during implementation:
- Original `MaterialSchemeHelper` has preference gates: secondary is ignored unless primary is enabled, and tertiary is ignored unless secondary is enabled.
- Rust `MaterialSchemes::from_seed_colors` currently accepts optional primary/secondary/tertiary directly; callers must enforce the same preference gates or the generated scheme can diverge.

## Component Mapping

### Application Shell

- Original: `ChoreoApp\App.xaml`, `ChoreoApp\App.xaml.cs`, `ChoreoApp\AppShell.xaml`, `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\Themes\Styles.xaml`, `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\Themes\MaterialDesign3.Window.xaml`
- Rust: `apps\desktop\src\main.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\app_shell\ui.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\choreo_main\ui.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\main_page\ui.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\shell\material_scheme_applier.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Window background: `Surface`
  - Application/Shell background: `Background`
  - Shell foreground/title: `OnBackground`
  - Shell disabled: `OutlineVariant`
  - Shell unselected: `OnSurfaceVariant`
  - Tab bar background: `Surface`
  - Tab selected foreground/title: `Primary`
  - Tab unselected: `OnSurfaceVariant`
- Rust colors found:
  - `app_shell::ui`: background `palette.background`
  - `choreo_main::ui`: modal overlay `background_modal`, dialog background `surface_container_low`, dialog text `on_surface`
  - `main_page::ui`: root fill `background`
  - `shell::material_scheme_applier`: only stores `background_hex` (`#FFFFFBFF` light, `#FF131318` dark), not the full scheme.
- Planning note:
  - Shell/root background should be reconciled against original distinction between `Background` for app shell and `Surface` for windows/content surfaces.
  - `shell::material_scheme_applier` is incomplete compared with original `MaterialDesignColorsDictionary`.

### Material Scheme / Palette

- Original: `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\MaterialDesignColorsDictionary.cs`, `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\MaterialDesignColorKey.cs`, `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\MaterialDesignColorsDictionary.Brushes.cs`
- Rust: `crates\material3\src\styling\material_schemes.rs`, `crates\material3\src\styling\material_palette.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Static Material 2 palettes are registered for red/green/blue/pink/purple/deep-purple/indigo/blue-grey/cyan/teal/light-green/lime/yellow/amber/orange/deep-orange/brown/gray plus black/white.
  - Dynamic keys set from `MaterialDynamicColors`: `Primary`, `Secondary`, `Tertiary`, `Neutral`, `NeutralVariant`, `Error`, all tone stops, and role colors `SurfaceTint`, `OnPrimary`, `PrimaryContainer`, `OnPrimaryContainer`, `OnSecondary`, `SecondaryContainer`, `OnSecondaryContainer`, `OnTertiary`, `TertiaryContainer`, `OnTertiaryContainer`, `OnError`, `ErrorContainer`, `OnErrorContainer`, `Background`, `OnBackground`, `Surface`, `OnSurface`, `SurfaceVariant`, `OnSurfaceVariant`, `Outline`, `OutlineVariant`, `Shadow`, `Scrim`, inverse colors, fixed colors, and surface container colors.
  - Brush lookup appends `Brush` to any color key and returns a cached `SolidColorBrush`.
- Rust colors found:
  - Dynamic scheme maps nearly the same role set into `MaterialScheme`.
  - `MaterialPalette` adds local derived values: `shadow_15`, `shadow_30`, `background_modal`, state-layer opacities, disabled opacity.
  - `sync_material_theme` exports role names to `egui_material3` global `selected_colors`.
- Planning note:
  - Rust does not appear to expose the original static Material color families in `material3`; domain colors exist in `choreo_models::Colors`, but Material UI token parity should be checked separately.

### Main Page / Navigation / Drawer

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\Main\MainPage.xaml`, `ChoreoApp.Components\Main\MainViewModel.cs`, Material drawer/navigation styles in `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\Themes\Styles.xaml`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\main_page\ui.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\main_page\top_bar.rs`, `crates\choreo_components\src\main_page\scene_pane.rs`, `crates\material3\src\components\drawer.rs`, `drawer_host`, `navigation_bar.rs`, `navigation_drawer.rs`, `navigation_rail.rs`, `top_bar_icon.rs`, `hamburger_toggle_button`
- Original colors:
  - Main content/drawer surfaces use `Surface` / `SurfaceContainerLow` depending style.
  - Navigation selected indicator: `SecondaryContainer` in Material navigation styles.
  - Navigation selected content: `OnSecondaryContainer`.
  - Navigation unselected content: `OnSurface` or `OnSurfaceVariant` depending control.
  - Hamburger bar: `OnSurface`; checked bar: `Primary`; disabled bar: `OnSurfaceVariant`; checked background: `SurfaceVariant`.
- Rust colors found:
  - `navigation_bar`: selected indicator `secondary_container`, selected content `on_secondary_container`, unselected content `on_surface`, bar fill `surface_container`.
  - `navigation_rail`: frame `surface`; selected content `on_secondary_container`, unselected `on_surface`, selected indicator `secondary_container`.
  - `navigation_drawer`: selected content `on_secondary_container`, unselected `on_surface_variant`, selected fill `secondary_container`.
  - `drawer`: title text `on_surface_variant`, panel fill `surface_container_low`, scrim `background_modal`.
  - `drawer_host::state` default is hard-coded overlay black alpha 102 and drawer background white until configured.
  - `hamburger_toggle_button`: normal bar `on_surface`, checked bar `secondary`, disabled bar `on_surface_variant`, checked background `secondary_container`; other backgrounds cycle through `surface_container_low/high/container`.
  - `top_bar_icon`: normal `on_surface`, selected `on_secondary_container`, disabled `on_surface * disabled_opacity`.
- Planning note:
  - Hamburger checked color likely diverges: original uses `Primary` and `SurfaceVariant`, Rust uses `Secondary` and `SecondaryContainer`.
  - Drawer host defaults should be driven by palette instead of white/black constants when used in app paths.

### Floor Canvas

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\Floor\FloorCanvasView.xaml(.cs)`, `ChoreoApp.Components\Floor\Behaviors\DrawFloorBehavior.cs`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\floor\ui.rs`, `floor_adapter.rs`, `floor_view_model.rs`, `floor\reducer.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Canvas clear/background fallback: `Surface`.
  - Grid: `Secondary`.
  - Title: `OnSurface`.
  - Subtitle and labels: `OnSurfaceVariant`.
  - Center mark and floor border: `Primary`.
  - Selection stroke around dancers: `Secondary`.
  - Missing/transparent position fill fallback: `SurfaceVariant` with choreography transparency.
  - Missing/transparent position border fallback: `OutlineVariant` with choreography transparency.
  - Position text auto-picks `White` or `Black` based on fill contrast.
  - Header/side panel border: `OutlineVariant`.
  - Empty/help overlays: `OnSurface`, `OnSurfaceVariant`, `SurfaceVariant` with alpha.
- Rust colors found:
  - Floor outside fill: `surface_container_highest`.
  - Floor border: `secondary` (2px).
  - Floor background: choreography `floor_color`.
  - Grid: `outline_variant`.
  - Front/axis/center markings: `secondary`, `primary`, `secondary`, `primary_container`.
  - Position selection: `secondary`; position border/fill/text come from reducer/model.
  - Empty/info overlay: `surface_container_low`, `on_surface`, `on_surface_variant`.
  - Header/settings strip: `surface_container`, `outline_variant`, `on_surface`, `on_surface_variant`, `secondary`.
- Planning note:
  - Clear mismatch candidates:
    - Original floor canvas background is `Surface`, Rust uses `SurfaceContainerHighest`.
    - Original grid is `Secondary`, Rust grid uses `OutlineVariant`.
    - Original floor border is `Primary`, Rust floor stroke uses `Secondary`.
    - Original transparent position fallback uses `SurfaceVariant`/`OutlineVariant`; Rust reducer fallback should be checked against this explicitly.

### Scenes Pane / Scene Item

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\Scenes\ScenesPaneView.xaml`, `SceneItemView.xaml`, `SceneItemViewStyles.xaml`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\scenes\ui.rs`, `main_page\scene_pane.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Pane background: `Surface`.
  - Scene item unselected background: `Surface`.
  - Scene item selected background: `PrimaryContainer`.
  - Unselected stroke: `OutlineVariant`.
  - Selected stroke: `Primary`.
  - Unselected text: `OnSurface`.
  - Selected text/timestamp: `OnPrimaryContainer`.
  - Unselected timestamp: `OnSurfaceVariant`.
  - Scene color swatch: bound scene color.
- Rust colors found:
  - Pane/card fills: `surface_container_low` and `surface_container`.
  - Strokes: `outline_variant`.
  - Scene text: `on_surface`; secondary/timestamp text: `on_surface_variant`.
  - Selected text helper returns `on_surface`; unselected returns `on_surface_variant` in one helper path.
- Planning note:
  - Scene selection styling is likely wrong/incomplete: original selected item must use `PrimaryContainer`, `Primary`, and `OnPrimaryContainer`, not just container surfaces and `OnSurface`.

### Audio Player

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\AudioPlayer\AudioPlayerView.xaml`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\audio_player\ui.rs`, `slider_with_ticks\ui.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Container background: `SurfaceContainer`.
  - Container stroke: `OutlineVariant`.
  - Time labels: `OnSurfaceVariant`.
  - Buttons/sliders use global Material styles.
- Rust colors found:
  - Slider tick color passed as `secondary`.
  - Other visible colors are mostly delegated to Material buttons/text/slider widgets.
- Planning note:
  - Verify audio container wrapper in Rust. The original explicit container is `SurfaceContainer` + `OutlineVariant`; if Rust lacks that wrapper or uses different fill, align it.

### Choreography Settings

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\ChoreographySettings\ChoreographySettingsView.xaml`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\choreography_settings\ui.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Uses Material controls and color pickers; floor/scene colors are bound data values.
  - Explicit material roles in the view should be checked line-by-line during implementation.
- Rust colors found:
  - Section/card fill: `surface_container`.
  - Section/card stroke: `outline_variant`.
  - Nested separators/borders: `outline_variant`.
  - Color values converted between model `Color` and `Color32`.
- Planning note:
  - Likely OK for generic sections if original uses Material cards/surfaces, but needs direct XAML comparison for selected-scene subpanel before coding.

### Dancers / Dancer Settings / Dancers Pane

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\Dancers\DancerSettingsPage.xaml`, `SwapDancersDialogView.xaml`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\dancers\ui.rs`, `dancer_settings_page\ui`, `dancers_pane_view`, `dancers\dancer_list_item_view`
- Original colors:
  - Swap dialog background: `Surface`.
  - Dialog text: `OnSurface`.
  - Dancer color swatches: bound dancer colors.
  - Generic inputs/buttons use Material styles.
- Rust colors found:
  - Dancer pane fill: `surface_container_low`, stroke `outline_variant`.
  - Dancer list selected: background `surface_container_high`, border `secondary`, text `on_surface`.
  - Dancer list unselected: background `surface_container_low`, border `outline_variant`, text `on_surface`, subtitle `on_surface_variant`.
  - Dancer swatches/icons use model colors.
- Planning note:
  - If original list selection uses Material selection colors elsewhere, Rust selected border `Secondary` should be verified. No original selected dancer style was found as clearly as scene item styles in this pass.

### Settings

- Original: `ChoreoApp.Components\Settings\SettingsPage.xaml`, `MaterialSchemeHelper.cs`
- Rust: `crates\choreo_components\src\settings\ui.rs`, settings behaviors/specs, `material_schemes.rs`
- Original colors:
  - Settings page background: `SurfaceContainerLow`.
  - Primary/secondary/tertiary color pickers bind user-selected colors.
  - Scheme preference gating as described above.
- Rust colors found:
  - Settings UI should be checked directly in implementation pass; current scan did not surface many explicit palette usages outside Material controls.
- Planning note:
  - Implementation should first test preference gating parity, then token assignment parity for any explicit settings page container.

### Generic Material Controls

- Original control styles live in `MaterialDesignThemes.Maui\Themes\MaterialDesign3.*.xaml`.
- Rust controls live in `crates\material3\src\components\*.rs`.

Observed direct mappings:
- Button:
  - Original default `Button`: background `Primary`, text `OnPrimary`; disabled background `SurfaceVariant`, disabled text `OnSurfaceVariant`.
  - Rust `FilledButton`: background `primary`, text `on_primary`; disabled text `on_surface`; no disabled background painted.
- Elevated button:
  - Rust: text `primary`; disabled text `on_surface`; background `surface_container_low`.
  - Need compare with original elevated style if present or add as explicit parity target.
- Tonal button:
  - Rust: background `secondary_container`, text `on_secondary_container`; disabled text `on_surface`.
  - Need compare with original tonal/content button styles.
- Text button:
  - Rust: text `primary` in light, `inverse_primary` in dark; disabled `on_surface`.
  - Dark-mode use of `inverse_primary` is suspicious; original Material text button styles likely use `Primary`.
- Icon button:
  - Rust checked/error/inverse/default paths use `error`, `inverse_on_surface`, `on_surface_variant`, `primary`.
  - Needs original `MaterialDesign3.ContentButton.xaml` / icon button comparison.
- Filled icon button:
  - Rust checked background `surface_container_highest`, unchecked background `primary`; checked content alternates `on_primary`/`primary`.
  - Needs original comparison.
- Tonal icon button:
  - Rust checked background `surface_container_highest`, unchecked background `secondary_container`, disabled background `surface`, checked content `on_secondary_container`, disabled content `on_surface_variant`.
  - Needs original comparison.
- Switch:
  - Rust selected track `primary`, unselected track `surface_container_highest`, outline `outline`, selected thumb `primary_container`, unselected thumb `outline`, selected icon/foreground `on_primary_container`, unselected foreground `surface_container_highest`, disabled opacity based on `on_surface`.
  - Original `MaterialDesign3.Switch.xaml` should be mapped exactly before code changes.
- Text field / drop-down / time picker:
  - Rust filled field background `surface_container_highest`; disabled/indicator/highlight use `on_surface` opacity, `error`, `primary`, `on_surface_variant`.
  - Original filled time picker uses `SurfaceContainerLow`; outlined uses `Surface`; underline/active use `Primary`.
  - Potential mismatch: `SurfaceContainerHighest` vs original `SurfaceContainerLow`.
- Card:
  - Rust elevated/filled/outlined variants use `surface`, `surface_container_highest`, `surface_container_low`, `outline`.
  - Original `MaterialDesign3.Card.xaml` should be checked for variant-specific tokens.
- Dialog:
  - Rust modal overlay `background_modal`, dialog fill `surface_container_high`, title/body `on_surface`.
  - Original dialogs should be compared against `DialogHost`/dialog XAML; app-specific dialogs often use `Surface`.
- Tooltip:
  - Rust `inverse_surface` + `inverse_on_surface`.
  - Likely correct Material mapping.
- Badge:
  - Rust `error` + `on_error`.
  - Likely correct Material mapping.
- Menu:
  - Rust menu frame `surface_container`, item hover `surface_container_highest`, text `on_surface`, secondary/disabled `on_surface_variant` or `on_surface * disabled_opacity`.
- List:
  - Rust text default `on_surface`, avatar default `primary` + `on_primary`.
  - Original `ListView` background `Surface`, separator `OutlineVariant`.

## Recommended Implementation Plan Later

1. Add focused specs for palette generation preference gating.
2. Add parity specs for the highest-risk visible components:
   - floor canvas
   - scene item selection
   - hamburger toggle / navigation
   - buttons disabled state
   - text field filled background
3. Fix token assignments in the narrowest modules first.
4. Run focused specs, then `cargo build`, `cargo clippy`, and `cargo test` for affected packages.

## Do Not Close Yet

This bead should remain open until the user confirms planning is sufficient and later implementation is complete.

Beads gitignore update: checked D:/beads current source. The canonical template lives in cmd/bd/doctor/gitignore.go and now ignores Dolt/runtime/export/backup artifacts such as embeddeddolt/, backup/, export-state/, *.lock, .sync.lock, last_pull, and .env. The source explicitly warns not to use negation patterns in .beads/.gitignore, so .beads/.gitignore was updated from the temporary blanket allowlist to the current canonical template plus a local RChoreoApp policy section ignoring top-level README.md, config.yaml, and metadata.json so future non-JSONL metadata is not added. Existing tracked non-JSONL .beads files still require a separate git rm --cached step if we want Git to stop versioning them.
Beads gitignore follow-up: D:/beads/cmd/bd/auto_import_upgrade.go defines .auto-import-issues.jsonl as an upgrade-recovery auto-import file. Added that exact filename to the local .beads/.gitignore policy section so generated auto-import JSONL does not appear as a versionable exchange file.

title: app_shell: replace AppShellViewModel with reducer-first shell state
description:
Goal:
Refactor the top-level app shell into a proper reducer-driven architecture so shell state, shell actions, and shell orchestration are explicit instead of being embedded in AppShellViewModel::ui.

Definition of Done:
- Introduce explicit app-shell state/actions/reducer modules or equivalent reducer-first structure.
- Move shell startup, splash, and routing orchestration out of AppShellViewModel mutation logic into reducer-driven flows.
- Keep egui/material/runtime integration in a thin shell runtime/coordinator only.
- Update tests to validate shell initialization, splash handoff, and routing through the new reducer pattern.
- Build, clippy, and tests pass for affected crates.

Notes:
Refactor the shell first so the remaining main-page coordinator can be removed from above rather than from underneath another controller-like layer.

title: choreo_main: replace main_page_binding with store and runtime split
description:
Goal:
Remove choreo_main/main_page_binding.rs as an architectural binding layer and replace it with a reducer-first pattern built around direct state ownership plus an explicit runtime/effect adapter.

Definition of Done:
- Replace MainPageBinding with a proper ChoreoMain store/runtime pattern or equivalent reducer-first structure.
- Keep ChoreoMainState ownership separate from host/runtime concerns such as file pickers, audio runtime, draw sender, and external open handlers.
- Preserve reducer orchestration via ChoreoMainAction handlers in *_behavior.rs files.
- Update app-shell and tests to consume the new store/runtime API directly.
- Build, clippy, and tests pass for affected crates.

Notes:
This follows the completed removal of MainViewModel/MainBehaviorPipeline and should eliminate the last coordinator-shaped layer inside choreo_main.

title: Refactor choreo_main to reducer-first action behaviors
description: Refactor choreo_main so the reducer is orchestration-only and action-specific behavior handlers contain the implementation details. Plan: 1) remove MainViewModel and MainViewModelProvider from the choreo_main execution path, replacing them with reducer-owned state plus a thin store/coordinator only if the UI boundary still needs one; 2) replace MainBehaviorPipeline/MainBehaviorDependencies with explicit ChoreoMain action handler dependencies organized by *_behavior.rs modules; 3) make reducer.rs dispatch into per-action behavior handlers instead of mixing direct branch implementations with runtime orchestration; 4) shrink MainPageBinding into a thin shell/runtime adapter or remove it where direct reducer access is sufficient; 5) update specs to validate the new reducer-first wiring and preserve startup/open/audio/svg behavior.

title: main_page_drawer_host: investigate drawer padding and hidden border

Definition of Done:
- Inventory controls/widgets/interactions in the original component.
- Verify egui implementation uses gui-material3 where original uses Material-style controls.
- Verify control semantics are preserved (for example icon/image buttons remain icon/image driven; no text-label substitutions that change intent).
- Verify layout/action behavior parity and interaction flow parity.
- Verify non-UI components do not gain GUI in the egui port.
- Document mismatches and create follow-up fix tickets if gaps are found.
- Do not modify crates/choreo_components.

Notes:
- UI expectation: Original component has UI; egui port must preserve control intent and use egui-material3 for Material controls where applicable.
- Scope: parity audit only for this component.
notes:
Parity audit summary (settings):
- Runtime/non-UI parity gap: original settings component exposes SettingsProvider + SettingsViewModel + behavior pipeline (load/switch/color/audio) and material updater integration; egui settings is reducer-centric without provider/behavior architecture parity.
- UI semantics gap: original settings page includes back navigation callback and can_use_system_theme capability gating; egui settings UI currently lacks explicit back-navigation action and lacks can_use_system_theme handling.
- Material3/translation parity gap: original settings UI is Material-based with translation-backed labels and material scheme helper updates; egui settings uses base egui widgets and hard-coded strings.
Follow-up tickets: RChoreoApp-jptn.15.1, RChoreoApp-jptn.15.2, RChoreoApp-jptn.15.3.

Follow-up tickets created:

- RChoreoApp-jptn.32 (non-UI API/pipeline parity)

title: docs: clean up egui.md wording and structure
description:
Goal:
Improve clarity and consistency in egui.md without changing technical intent.
Definition of Done:
- egui.md wording/formatting is cleaned for readability and consistency
- No technical behavior or requirements changed
- Only egui.md modified
Notes:
User requested file-scoped documentation cleanup only.

title: nav_bar: add floor viewport reset button
description:
Goal:
Implement a nav bar button that resets the user floor viewport transform (zoom and pan) to defaults.
Definition of Done:
- A reset button is visible in the nav bar when floor view is active.
- Clicking the button resets floor zoom to default and pan offsets to default center/origin state.
- Existing floor interactions keep working after reset.
- Behavior is covered by specs.
Notes:
- Wire through View-ViewModel adapter and behavior/message pipeline per project architecture.

title: floor: add two-finger pinch and pan gestures
description:
Goal:
Implement two-finger touch gestures for floor zoom and pan.
Definition of Done:
- Pinch gesture updates zoom smoothly within configured min/max bounds.
- Two-finger drag pans the floor viewport.
- Gesture math is stable and does not jump when fingers are added/removed.
- Behavior is covered by specs for zoom and pan updates.
Notes:
- Keep desktop mouse interactions unchanged and compatible.

title: floor: reset viewport on double tap gesture
description:
Goal:
Allow users to reset floor zoom/pan by double tapping the floor view.
Definition of Done:
- Double tap gesture on floor view triggers the same reset action as the reset button.
- Gesture does not interfere with single tap select/place interactions.
- Behavior is covered by specs, including interaction edge cases.
Notes:
- Reuse shared reset command/path instead of duplicating logic.

title: audio_player: improve slider tick visibility
description:
Goal:
Make AudioPlayer SliderWithTicks scene timestamp markers clearly visible.
Definition of Done:
- Tick marker height/width increased for better visibility
- Tick marker color adjusted for higher contrast
- build/clippy/tests run for choreo_components
Notes:
Focus on audio player slider usage and shared slider_with_ticks rendering.
notes:
Audit Findings (2026-02-08):
1) High - Play/pause flicker possible due to optimistic UI state writes before actor confirmation (main_page_binding.rs:1482, audio_player_view_model.rs:121, main_page_binding.rs:661).
2) High - Slider ownership can get stuck in user-interaction mode if drag-complete is missed; actor sync remains blocked (main_page_binding.rs:661, audio_player_view_model.rs:259, slider_with_ticks.slint:36/130/142).
3) High - Latest-event-wins violated by command drop on try_borrow_mut failure in open/close/link behaviors (open_audio_file_behavior.rs:66, close_audio_file_behavior.rs:40, audio_player_link_scene_behavior.rs:57).
4) Medium - Duplicate ownership for tick/link state between main_page_binding timer and AudioPlayerTicksBehavior (main_page_binding.rs:672, audio_player_ticks_behavior.rs:51).
5) Medium - Link-scene bus path is bypassed by direct UI mutation in binding (main_page_binding.rs:1461) while view-model/behavior path also exists.
6) Medium - Position publish behavior uses borrow() (panic risk) instead of non-failing access (audio_player_position_changed_behavior.rs:45).

Plan Alignment Confirmed:
- SeekAndPlay(abs_pos) is atomic in actor.
- Coalescing preserves arrival order via sequence index.
- Slider emits position_changed only from user-driven set_value.

Replace any subject-based or direct view model borrowing patterns in choreo_main behaviors with channel-based events and injected behaviors.
Definition of Done:
choreo_main behavior files avoid public handler methods and avoid borrowing ViewModel in callbacks; uses Sender/Receiver as needed; build/clippy/test pass for choreo_components.
Notes:
Focus on crates/choreo_components/src/choreo_main/*.rs behaviors.

title: floor: refactor gesture handling behavior
description:
Goal:
Replace gesture handling subjects with crossbeam channels, remove public methods on behavior, and inject behaviors in FloorViewModel.
Definition of Done:
Gesture events use Sender/Receiver, no view model borrow panic, behaviors injected, floor_view_model updated accordingly.
Notes:
Update gesture_handling_behavior.rs and floor_view_model.rs as needed.

title: tooling: investigate cargo incremental cleanup access denied warning
description:
Goal:
Investigate why cargo build/test intermittently logs rror finalizing incremental compilation session directory ... Access is denied. (os error 5) on Windows and document/implement a stable fix.
Definition of Done:
- Reproduce warning reliably on Windows dev environment.
- Identify root cause (process lock, antivirus/indexer, temp permissions, or build configuration).
- Add mitigation and verify warning no longer appears in repeated cargo build -p choreo_components_egui and affected tests.
Notes:
Observed while validating RChoreoApp-jptn.26; behavior/runtime parity changes are not dependent on this warning.
